attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- commit: Stage and commit changes with a conventional commit message
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- triage: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
- prototype: Build a throwaway prototype to flush out a design before committing to it. Routes between two branches — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route. Use when the user wants to prototype, sanity-check a data model or state machine, mock up a UI, explore design options, or says "prototype this", "let me play with it", "try a few designs".
- diagnose: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
- to-issues: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices. Use when user wants to convert a plan into issues, create implementation tickets, or break down work into issues.
- write-a-skill: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- to-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
The homepage is currently just `featured banner + one trending grid`, and that grid is hardcoded to `category: "skills"` (page.tsx:26) even though the section is labeled generically — so "trending" silently hides MCP/commands/plugins. The biggest payoff would be **fixing that filter and adding per-category sections** (Skills / MCP / Commands / Plugins, each a small row) so the marketplace's breadth is visible above the fold; a lighter alternative is to keep one grid but make it truly cross-category and add a search input to the hero.

Tradeoff: per-category rows means more DB calls and a taller page; cross-category single grid is cheaper but less of a "browse" experience. Which direction do you want — fix-the-bug-only, multi-row browse, or hero-with-search?

content.text:
Clean. Here's what changed in `app/[locale]/page.tsx`:

- **Two-column grid** (`md:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_auto]`) — text on the left, hero icon tile on the right, vertically centered.
- **Hero icon tile** — `size-44` (~176px) rounded square reusing the extension's `iconEmoji` + `iconColor` (same recipe as `ext-card.tsx`, but scaled up). Subtle hover lift+tilt, soft primary-tinted shadow. Wrapped in a Link to the extension page.
- **CTA + stats row** under the description — `View extension →` link in primary color (arrow shifts on hover) and a mono-font installs counter with a download icon, echoing the eyebrow's mono treatment.
- **Decorative blur retint** — second blob now uses the featured extension's `iconColor` so the ambient color harmonizes with the icon tile instead of fading into nothing. First blob enlarged and pushed slightly to better frame the icon.
- **i18n** — added `viewExtension` and `installsCount` keys to both `en.json` and `zh.json`.

The right side is now anchored by the icon and the left column gets a proper foot (CTA + stat) instead of trailing into white space.

Agreed — a giant emoji clashes with the Editorial Ivory typography. Two ways out, both more in keeping with the magazine-feel aesthetic:

1. **Remove it entirely** — let the right side breathe as editorial whitespace, keep just the soft blurred color washes and the CTA + install count we already added on the left. Simplest, most refined.
2. **Replace it with a display number** — render the install count (or rating) as a large serif-italic numeral on the right with a small mono caption underneath, like a magazine pull-stat. Fills the space *typographically* instead of with iconography.

I'd lean toward **#1**: the original "empty space" wasn't really empty, just unanchored, and the new CTA row already gives the left column a foot. Want me to try that, or go with the display-number version?

message.content: may be we can put the description and under info to right side, improve the layout of featured banner. what do you think?

content.text:
Good direction — that's a classic editorial spread: eyebrow + serif headline on the left as the typographic anchor, description + CTA on the right as the supporting column. Lets the title grow larger (e.g. `text-5xl`) since it no longer has the description stacked beneath it, and the right column can use `flex-col justify-between` so the description sits at the top and the CTA sits at the bottom — vertically balancing the title's height.

Tradeoff: at narrow widths it collapses back to stacked (so we'd keep the order title → description → CTA on mobile), and short titles + short descriptions could leave a slightly empty middle. Want me to try it, with the title bumped to `text-5xl`?

content.text:
Clean. Here's what the new banner does:

**Editorial three-band composition** (top → middle → bottom):

- **Top band**: a thin horizontal rule split by the eyebrow label "FEATURED THIS WEEK" centered between the rules — looks like a magazine section divider. Eyebrow tracking widened slightly (`0.22em`) for refined caps spacing.
- **Middle band** (the focal point): centered serif title at `text-5xl`/`text-[64px]` with tight `leading-[1.04]`, italic-accented last word kept. Below it, a centered italic-serif "deck" sentence (max-w-xl) — that *italic serif* body treatment is a magazine convention that signals "this is editorial copy," lifting the description from regular UI text.
- **Bottom band**: thin border-top rule, then asymmetric footer — mono uppercase `↓ 12.4k installs` on the left (newspaper byline feel), primary `View extension →` on the right.

**Ambient color**: the two blurs were repositioned to center-top and center-bottom (replacing the right-anchored ones) so the soft color washes now frame the title symmetrically. Second blur still tints to the featured extension's `iconColor` for subtle harmonization.

Take a look in the browser — the "centered hero with rules" pattern stands or falls on actual proportions, so let me know what you think.

message.content: 2. make the extension's card clickable, not only click the title jump to the detail page. what do you think?

content.text:
Good idea — the standard pattern for this is the "stretched link": keep the title `<Link>` but give it an `::after` pseudo-element absolutely positioned over the whole card, so the entire card surface becomes the link's hit area. The Save/Install buttons inside the card stay clickable by sitting on a higher stacking context (`position: relative z-10`). This preserves semantic HTML (one anchor per card, buttons remain buttons — no nested interactive elements) and accessibility (screen readers see one link, focus order stays sane).

Tradeoff: text selection inside the card becomes slightly trickier because the overlay sits on top of the description and tags — users can still drag-select but it requires a more deliberate gesture. Want me to implement it on `components/extension/ext-card.tsx`?

content.text:
All tests pass. Card is now fully clickable:

- **`relative`** added to `<article>` so the title link's `::after` positions against the card.
- **`after:absolute after:inset-0 after:content-['']`** on the title `<Link>` stretches its hit area over the entire card. The visible link text is unchanged; the pseudo-element is invisible but captures clicks across the card surface.
- **`relative z-10`** on the action buttons row so Save/Install sit above the overlay and stay independently clickable.

Tags, the dept badge, the icon tile, description, and the stars/downloads metadata stay below the overlay (they're non-interactive, so it's fine — clicks anywhere on them now navigate to the detail page). Hovering anywhere on the card still triggers the existing translate/shadow hover, and the title turns primary because the cursor is over the link's hit area — visual feedback that the whole card is one target.

Want this committed on the same branch?

22	// via sql``. It is GENERATED ALWAYS (read-only); never written by app code.

24	  dataType() { return "tsvector"; },
25	});

41	  "official",

44	]);
45

47	  "workTask",
48	  "business",
49	  "tools",
50	]);
51	
52	export const visibilityEnum = pgEnum("extension_visibility", [
53	  "draft",
54	  "published",
55	  "archived",
56	]);
57	
58	export const versionStatusEnum = pgEnum("version_status", [
59	  "pending",
60	  "scanning",
61	  "ready",
62	  "rejected",
63	]);
64	
65	export const fileScanStatusEnum = pgEnum("file_scan_status", [
66	  "pending",
67	  "clean",
68	  "flagged",
69	]);
70	
71	export const extensions = pgTable(
72	  "extensions",
73	  {
74	    id: text().primaryKey(),
75	    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
76	    category: extensionCategoryEnum().notNull(),
77	    badge: extensionBadgeEnum(),
78	    scope: extensionScopeEnum().notNull(),
79	    // funcCat/subCat are nullable — the redesigned publish wizard does not
80	    // collect them; admin curation can backfill or system defaults apply.
81	    funcCat: funcCatEnum(),
82	    subCat: text(),
83	    l2: text(),
84	    publisherUserId: text().references(() => users.id, {
85	      onDelete: "set null",
86	    }),
87	    ownerOrgId: text()
88	      .notNull()
89	      .references(() => organizations.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
90	    deptId: text().references(() => departments.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
91	    iconEmoji: text(),
92	    iconColor: text(),
93	    visibility: visibilityEnum().notNull().default("draft"),
94	    // i18n columns
95	    name: text().notNull(),
96	    nameZh: text(),
97	    tagline: text(),
98	    taglineZh: text(),
99	    description: text(),
100	    descriptionZh: text(),
101	    // Long-form markdown body for the detail page. Not duplicated to ZH yet —
102	    // most extensions will have an English README, with translations layered
103	    // on later if needed.
104	    readmeMd: text(),
105	    // metadata for detail page sidebar
106	    homepageUrl: text(),
107	    repoUrl: text(),
108	    licenseSpdx: text(),
109	    compatibilityJson: jsonb(),
110	    // Permissions captured during publish (network/files/runtime/data, etc.).
111	    // Surfaced on the detail page so users see what the extension wants.
112	    permissions: jsonb().notNull().default({}),
113	    // denormalized counters (updated by jobs)
114	    downloadsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
115	    starsAvg: numeric({ precision: 2, scale: 1 }).notNull().default("0.0"),
116	    ratingsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
117	    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
118	    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
119	    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
120	    searchVector: tsvector("search_vector"),
121	  },
122	  (t) => [
123	    index("idx_ext_category").on(t.category),
124	    index("idx_ext_scope").on(t.scope),
125	    index("idx_ext_func_sub_l2").on(t.funcCat, t.subCat, t.l2),
126	    index("idx_ext_dept_path").using(
127	      "btree",
128	      sql`${t.deptId} text_pattern_ops`,
129	    ),
130	    index("idx_ext_visibility").on(t.visibility),
131	    index("idx_ext_downloads").on(sql`${t.downloadsCount} DESC`),
132	    index("idx_ext_stars").on(sql`${t.starsAvg} DESC`),
133	  ],
134	);
135	
136	export const extensionVersions = pgTable(
137	  "extension_versions",
138	  {
139	    id: text().primaryKey(),
140	    extensionId: text()
141	      .notNull()
142	      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
143	    version: text().notNull(), // semver
144	    changelog: text(),
145	    changelogZh: text(),
146	    manifestJson: jsonb(),
147	    // Logical reference to files.id; no FK constraint to avoid a circular FK
148	    // with files.extensionVersionId. App code keeps these consistent.
149	    bundleFileId: text(),
150	    status: versionStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
151	    // Source method chosen during publish: zip (only one wired today), git, cli.
152	    // Stored as text rather than enum so adding methods doesn't require a migration.
153	    sourceMethod: text().notNull().default("zip"),
154	    sourceMeta: jsonb().notNull().default({}),
155	    publishedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
156	    createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
157	  },

159	);
160	
161	export const files = pgTable("files", {
162	  id: text().primaryKey(),
163	  extensionVersionId: text().references(() => extensionVersions.id, {
164	    onDelete: "set null",

166	  r2Key: text().notNull(),
167	  size: bigint({ mode: "bigint" }).notNull(),
168	  checksumSha256: text().notNull(),
169	  mimeType: text(),
170	  scanStatus: fileScanStatusEnum().notNull().default("pending"),
171	  scanReport: jsonb(),
172	  createdAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
173	});
174	
175	export const tags = pgTable("tags", {
176	  id: text().primaryKey(), // tag key, e.g. "real-time"
177	  labelEn: text().notNull(),
178	  labelZh: text().notNull(),
179	});
180	
181	export const extensionTags = pgTable(
182	  "extension_tags",
183	  {
184	    extensionId: text()
185	      .notNull()
186	      .references(() => extensions.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
187	    tagId: text()
188	      .notNull()
189	      .references(() => tags.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
190	  },

192	    primaryKey({ columns: [t.extensionId, t.tagId] }),
193	    index("idx_extension_tags_tag_ext").on(t.tagId, t.extensionId),
194	  ],
195	);

1	import { and, desc, eq, ilike, inArray, like, or, type SQL, sql } from "drizzle-orm";
2	
3	import { MY_DEPT_ID } from "@/lib/data/departments"; // fallback for unauthenticated users
4	import { db } from "@/lib/db/client";
5	import { extensions, extensionTags } from "@/lib/db/schema";
6	import type { Filters } from "@/lib/validators/filters";
7	
8	// Special token in the dept URL param meaning "show all departments".
9	const ALL_DEPTS = "__all";
10	
11	/**
12	 * Compose a SQL WHERE expression from typed filters. Returns `undefined` when
13	 * no clauses are active so callers can omit `.where()` entirely.
14	 */
15	export function buildExtensionWhere(
16	  filters: Filters,
17	  fallbackDeptId?: string,
18	): SQL | undefined {
19	  const clauses = [
20	    // Always limit to published extensions.
21	    eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
22	
23	    filters.category ? eq(extensions.category, filters.category) : undefined,
24	    filters.scope ? eq(extensions.scope, filters.scope) : undefined,
25	    filters.funcCat ? eq(extensions.funcCat, filters.funcCat) : undefined,
26	    filters.subCat ? eq(extensions.subCat, filters.subCat) : undefined,
27	    filters.l2 ? eq(extensions.l2, filters.l2) : undefined,
28	
29	    // Dotted-path descendant filter — uses idx_ext_dept_path (text_pattern_ops).
30	    // When no dept param is provided, fall back to the authenticated user's
31	    // defaultDeptId (passed in from the page via getSession), then to MY_DEPT_ID
32	    // for unauthenticated visitors.
33	    (() => {
34	      const dept = filters.dept ?? fallbackDeptId ?? MY_DEPT_ID;
35	      if (dept === ALL_DEPTS) return undefined;
36	      return or(
37	        eq(extensions.deptId, dept),
38	        like(extensions.deptId, `${dept}.%`),
39	      );
40	    })(),
41	
42	    // Filter chips
43	    filters.filter === "trending"
44	      ? sql`${extensions.downloadsCount} > 50000`
45	      : undefined,
46	    filters.filter === "new" ? eq(extensions.badge, "new") : undefined,
47	    filters.filter === "official"
48	      ? eq(extensions.badge, "official")
49	      : undefined,
50	    filters.filter === "free"
51	      ? sql`${extensions.licenseSpdx} IS NOT NULL`
52	      : undefined,
53	
54	    // FTS via tsvector (set weight A/B/C in the GENERATED column) with
55	    // pg_trgm ILIKE fallback for short queries and CJK substrings.
56	    filters.q
57	      ? or(
58	          sql`${extensions.searchVector} @@ websearch_to_tsquery('simple', ${filters.q})`,
59	          ilike(extensions.name, `%${filters.q}%`),
60	          ilike(extensions.nameZh, `%${filters.q}%`),
61	        )
62	      : undefined,
63	
64	    // Tag filter via aggregating subquery — `any` matches if at least one tag
65	    // overlaps; `all` requires every selected tag to be present.
66	    filters.tags && filters.tags.length > 0
67	      ? inArray(
68	          extensions.id,
69	          db
70	            .select({ id: extensionTags.extensionId })
71	            .from(extensionTags)
72	            .where(inArray(extensionTags.tagId, filters.tags))
73	            .groupBy(extensionTags.extensionId)
74	            .having(
75	              filters.tagMatch === "all"
76	                ? sql`count(*) = ${filters.tags.length}`
77	                : sql`count(*) >= 1`,
78	            ),
79	        )
80	      : undefined,
81	  ].filter((c): c is SQL => Boolean(c));
82	
83	  return clauses.length > 0 ? and(...clauses) : undefined;
84	}
85	
86	export function buildExtensionOrder(sort: Filters["sort"]) {
87	  switch (sort) {
88	    case "stars":
89	      return [desc(extensions.starsAvg), desc(extensions.downloadsCount)];
90	    case "recent":
91	      return [desc(extensions.publishedAt), desc(extensions.createdAt)];
92	    case "downloads":
93	    default:
94	      return [desc(extensions.downloadsCount)];
95	  }
96	}
97

content.text: [Request interrupted by user]

Here's what I found and what I'd propose. Stopping to align before writing code.

## Existing data model already covers both

- `extensions.publisherUserId` → users.id — this *is* your "**creator**" (the user who published). Just named misleadingly in the schema.
- `extensions.ownerOrgId` → organizations.id — this *is* your "**publisher**" (the org).
- `memberships` (user ↔ org, unique on `(userId, orgId)`) already permits one user to belong to multiple orgs — your "one creator → many publishers" rule.

So **no migrations needed**. We can leave the schema column named `publisherUserId` (renaming is invasive churn) and just expose it as "creator" everywhere user-facing.

## Proposed implementation (4 commits)

1. **Filter validators + WHERE clauses** — add `creator` and `publisher` to `filtersSchema` (zod), and two `eq(...)` clauses in `buildExtensionWhere`. Trivial.
2. **Lookup queries** — `listPublishedCreators()` and `listPublishedPublishers()` returning `{id, name, extensionCount}` sorted by count desc, used to populate dropdowns. Joined to `users`/`organizations`, restricted to extensions with `visibility='published'`.
3. **Filter UI** — two new client components (`CreatorPicker`, `PublisherPicker`) modeled on `DeptPicker`: popover trigger + searchable list. Wire into `FilterBar` between dept and scope. Add a chip in `FilterChips` showing the active selection with a clear-x.
4. **i18n + URL wiring** — `?creator=<userId>&publisher=<orgId>`, English/Chinese strings, single-select on both for now.

## Open questions before I start

1. **Single-select or multi-select?** Existing dept/scope are single, tags are multi. I'd start single (simpler URL, simpler dropdown); we can extend later.
2. **Do the seeds populate `publisherUserId` / `ownerOrgId`?** If not, the filter will look empty in dev — I'll check and add seed coverage if missing.
3. **Where in the bar?** Suggest adding them on the same row as `DeptPicker` (left → right: Dept · Publisher · Creator · Scope).

Want me to go ahead with single-select + that placement, or change something first?

140	
141	export type ExtensionDetail = NonNullable<
142	  Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getExtensionBySlug>>
143	>;
144	
145	export async function getLatestExtensionVersion(extensionId: string) {
146	  const [row] = await db
147	    .select({
148	      version: extensionVersions.version,
149	      changelog: extensionVersions.changelog,
150	      changelogZh: extensionVersions.changelogZh,
151	      publishedAt: extensionVersions.publishedAt,
152	      bundleSize: files.size,
153	    })
154	    .from(extensionVersions)
155	    .leftJoin(files, eq(files.id, extensionVersions.bundleFileId))
156	    .where(
157	      and(
158	        eq(extensionVersions.extensionId, extensionId),
159	        eq(extensionVersions.status, "ready"),
160	      ),
161	    )
162	    .orderBy(desc(extensionVersions.publishedAt))
163	    .limit(1);
164	  return row ?? null;
165	}
166	
167	export async function listExtensionVersions(extensionId: string) {
168	  return db
169	    .select({
170	      version: extensionVersions.version,
171	      changelog: extensionVersions.changelog,
172	      changelogZh: extensionVersions.changelogZh,
173	      publishedAt: extensionVersions.publishedAt,
174	    })
175	    .from(extensionVersions)
176	    .where(
177	      and(
178	        eq(extensionVersions.extensionId, extensionId),
179	        eq(extensionVersions.status, "ready"),
180	      ),
181	    )
182	    .orderBy(desc(extensionVersions.publishedAt))
183	    .limit(20);
184	}
185	
186	export type ExtensionVersionRow = Awaited<
187	  ReturnType<typeof listExtensionVersions>
188	>[number];
189	
190	export async function getRelatedExtensions(
191	  extensionId: string,
192	  category: ExtensionDetail["category"],
193	  limit = 4,
194	) {
195	  return db
196	    .select({
197	      id: extensions.id,
198	      slug: extensions.slug,
199	      name: extensions.name,
200	      nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
201	      iconEmoji: extensions.iconEmoji,
202	      iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
203	      starsAvg: extensions.starsAvg,
204	      downloadsCount: extensions.downloadsCount,
205	    })
206	    .from(extensions)
207	    .where(
208	      and(
209	        eq(extensions.category, category),
210	        eq(extensions.visibility, "published"),
211	        ne(extensions.id, extensionId),
212	      ),
213	    )
214	    .orderBy(desc(extensions.downloadsCount))
215	    .limit(limit);
216	}
217	
218	export type RelatedExtension = Awaited<
219	  ReturnType<typeof getRelatedExtensions>
220	>[number];
221	
222	export async function countPublishedExtensions() {
223	  const [row] = await db
224	    .select({ count: count() })
225	    .from(extensions)
226	    .where(eq(extensions.visibility, "published"));
227	  return Number(row?.count ?? 0);
228	}

content.text:
Heads up: this work is unrelated to homepage improvements — should we move it to a fresh branch like `feat/creator-publisher-filters` so the PRs stay separate? Either way, the work splits cleanly into three coherent commits:

1. **`feat(seed): add creator users and per-author publisher orgs`** — `scripts/seed.ts` only (mock creators, per-author org rows, derived memberships, extensions now route to a creator + non-default org).
2. **`feat(filters): creator/publisher filter schema, queries, WHERE clauses`** — `lib/validators/filters.ts`, `lib/search/query.ts`, `lib/db/queries/facets.ts`.

input.command:
git add scripts/seed.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(seed): add creator users and per-author publisher orgs

Six mock creator users (idempotent insert) become extension publishers,
and each unique extension `author` is materialized as its own org so the
publisher filter has real options. Extensions are deterministically
assigned a creator (round-robin by index) and routed to the org matching
their author. Memberships are then derived from the distinct
(creator, owning org) pairs that emerge — so most creators end up in
several orgs, satisfying the "one creator can belong to multiple
publishers" rule organically.
EOF
)"

clauses in buildExtensionWhere — creator maps to extensions.publisherUserId
(the existing column, named for legacy reasons but exposed as "creator"
in user-facing code), publisher maps to extensions.ownerOrgId.

New queries module `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` returns
{id, name, count} rows for each, restricted to visibility='published'
and ordered by extension count desc, used to populate the picker
dropdowns.
EOF
)"

git add components/layout/sidebar.tsx lib/i18n/messages/en.json lib/i18n/messages/zh.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(sidebar): drop "Installed" pseudo-collection

The sidebar's Collections section had two static placeholders ("Installed"
and "Saved"), both wired to a hard-coded count of 0. "Installed" isn't a
real collection — install state lives on the extension, and the saved
list already covers the bookmark-style use case. Removes the entry plus
its sidebar.installed translations in en/zh.
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Home improvements: editorial banner, clickable cards, creator/publisher filters" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Branch bundles a few related home/extensions improvements:

- **Featured banner redesign** — replaced the lopsided card (lots of empty whitespace on the right, then a too-big emoji tile) with a magazine-style editorial composition: horizontal rules framing a centered serif headline + italic deck, footer rule with mono installs counter and "View extension →" CTA. No iconography.
- **Whole extension card clickable** — stretched-link pattern on `ext-card.tsx`: the title `<Link>` grows an `::after` overlay covering the card. Save/Install buttons sit on `relative z-10` and stay independently clickable. Semantics unchanged (one anchor + two buttons, no nested interactives).
- **Creator and Publisher filters** on `/extensions` — single-select pickers in `FilterBar`. Creator → `extensions.publisherUserId` (existing column, just exposed as "creator" in user-facing code). Publisher → `extensions.ownerOrgId`. New `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` returns `{id, name, count}` lookups for the dropdowns. Seed now creates 6 mock creator users and turns each unique extension `author` into its own publisher org, with memberships derived from the (creator, owning org) pairs that emerge — so most creators end up in several orgs ("one creator can belong to multiple publishers" satisfied organically).
- **i18n polish** — 技能 → Skill and MCP 服务器 / MCP 服务 → MCP everywhere in zh.json, with CJK-typography spacing (热门 Skill, 搜索 Skill、MCP…). 斜杠命令 and 插件 stay in Chinese.
- **Sidebar cleanup** — dropped the "Installed" pseudo-collection (was a hard-coded 0; install state belongs on the extension, not as a fake collection).

## Test plan

- [ ] Re-run `bun run db:seed` against your local DB so the new creators / publisher orgs / memberships exist; existing extensions migrate to non-default org.
- [ ] Open `/` (both `/en` and `/zh`) and verify the featured banner renders as the editorial composition (rules + centered title + footer strip).
- [ ] Hover anywhere on a trending card and click — should navigate to the detail page. Then verify Save and Install still work without navigating.
- [ ] Open `/extensions` — Publisher and Creator pickers appear next to Department; selecting one narrows results, the URL updates with `?creator=…` / `?publisher=…`, and selections survive a refresh.
- [ ] Switch to `/zh` and confirm `Skill` / `MCP` show as English in category labels and inside the search placeholder; spacing reads naturally.
- [ ] Sidebar Collections section shows `Saved` (no `Installed`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough This PR adds faceted filtering by creator and publisher to the extension marketplace. It introduces database queries for aggregating published creator and publisher data, new filter picker components with popover UI, validation schema extensions, search query integration, and updated seeding logic to generate deterministic test data with multi-organization ownership relationships. ## Changes **Creator and Publisher Filtering** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Facet Data Contracts**
`lib/db/queries/facets.ts`|New module exports `listPublishedCreators()` and `listPublishedPublishers()` functions that aggregate extension counts by creator (user) and publisher (organization) for published extensions, plus derived `CreatorFacet` and `PublisherFacet` TypeScript types.| |**Filter Validation Schema**
`lib/validators/filters.ts`|Schema extended with optional `creator` and `publisher` fields as trimmed strings with length bounds; semantics clarified with inline comments.| |**Search Query Integration**
`lib/search/query.ts`|`buildExtensionWhere` now adds optional SQL equality predicates for `filters.creator` (matched to `extensions.publisherUserId`) and `filters.publisher` (matched to `extensions.ownerOrgId`).| |**Creator Picker Component**
`components/filters/creator-picker.tsx`|New client-side component renders popover-based creator selector with label formatting (name or email), active state tracking via `useFilters()`, and empty-state messaging.| |**Publisher Picker Component**
`components/filters/publisher-picker.tsx`|New client-side component renders popover-based publisher selector with locale-aware label formatting, filter state integration, and option counting.| |**FilterBar Integration**
`components/filters/filter-bar.tsx`|Component signature and props extended to accept `creators` and `publishers` arrays; renders new `CreatorPicker` and `PublisherPicker` alongside existing filter controls.| |**ExtensionsPage Data Fetching**
`app/[locale]/extensions/page.tsx`|Imports new facet query functions and extends `Promise.all` to fetch `creators` and `publishers`; passes both into `FilterBar` component.| |**ExtCard Layout Polish**
`components/extension/ext-card.tsx`|Root article gains `relative` positioning; name link gains `after:` pseudo-element overlay; actions wrapper updated with `relative z-10` stacking.| |**Home Page Featured Section**
`app/[locale]/page.tsx`|Adds `Download` icon import and internal `formatCount()` helper; refactors featured extension markup/styles with `Download` icon and formatted install count display.| |**Sidebar Cleanup**
`components/layout/sidebar.tsx`|Removes "installed" placeholder from collections section; converts placeholder rows to non-focusable `div` with `aria-disabled="true"`.| |**Internationalization**
`lib/i18n/messages/en.json`, `lib/i18n/messages/zh.json`|English translations add creator/publisher filter groups and new home display keys; Chinese localization refreshed with market terminology ("Skill", "MCP") throughout home, categories, sidebar, dashboard, and wizard sections; "installed" sidebar key removed.| |**Test Data Generation**
`scripts/seed.ts`|Extended with deterministic `CREATORS` array and per-author `ORGANIZATIONS`; extension seeding now assigns `publisherUserId` and `ownerOrgId`; adds membership seeding for creator-organization pairs.| ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~25 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [ainaive/agentcenter#16](https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter/pull/16): Updates sidebar placeholders and i18n filter/sidebar keys similarly, including aria-disabled changes. - [ainaive/agentcenter#15](https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter/pull/15): Modifies `app/[locale]/extensions/page.tsx` and FilterBar prop wiring for facet data display. - [ainaive/agentcenter#2](https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter/pull/2): Touches sidebar and locale JSON translations with similar terminology updates. ## Poem > 🐰 *Creators and publishers now shine so bright,* > *Faceted filters help users find just right,* > *Pickers pop open with organized cheer,* > *Downloads counted in thousands up here,* > *Skill and MCP dance through every screen—* > *The finest extension marketplace you've seen!* ✨

content.text:
CodeRabbit left two nitpicks (both 🟡 minor):

**1. `app/[locale]/page.tsx:47` — invalid CSS when `iconColor` is null.**

```tsx
background: `${featured.iconColor ?? "var(--primary)"}18`,
```

The `18` is a hex alpha suffix — it only works on hex colors. If `iconColor` is null, the fallback produces `var(--primary)18`, which is invalid CSS. In practice every seeded extension sets `iconColor`, so this is defensive only, but real if a future featured row lands with a null. Suggested fix: only render that second blur when `iconColor` is set.

**2. `scripts/seed.ts:150` — duplicate org slugs.**

If two distinct `author` strings slugify to the same value, `db.insert(organizations)` fails on the unique `slug` constraint. Current 15 authors all collide-free, so it's also defensive only — but the failure mode is silent during normal dev work and noisy on first seed. Suggested fix: track a `Set<string>` and append `-1`, `-2` on collision.

Both worth applying — small, defensive. Want me to fix both and push?

content.content: lib/auth/client.ts:4:  baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000",

toolUseResult.stdout: lib/auth/client.ts:4:  baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000",

content.text:
Diagnosis: the auth client falls back to localhost when `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` is missing.

**`lib/auth/client.ts:4`**
```ts
baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000",
```

In Vercel, if you didn't set `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`, every browser-side signup POST goes to `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/...` — which works on your laptop but is unreachable from a visitor's browser, so the request silently fails.

## Likely missing Vercel env vars

Set these in Vercel → Project → Settings → Environment Variables (Production):

1. **`NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=https://agentcenter.vercel.app`** ← almost certainly the cause
2. **`BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`** — a random 32+ char string (Better Auth signs session cookies with this; without it sessions don't persist between cold starts)
3. **`DATABASE_URL`** — verify it's set to the Neon prod connection string (not the local one)

After updating env vars, redeploy. `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` are baked into the client bundle at build time, so a redeploy is required for the change to reach the browser.

## A code change worth considering

The localhost fallback hides this exact class of bug. I'd suggest replacing it so the production build fails fast:

```ts
baseURL:
  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ??
  (process.env.NODE_ENV === "production"
    ? (() => { throw new Error("NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL must be set in production"); })()
    : "http://localhost:3000"),
```

Or simpler: drop the `baseURL` entirely and let Better Auth's client default to `window.location.origin` — that way it just works wherever it's hosted.

Want me to apply the simpler `window.location.origin` fix on a branch, or just confirm the env-var change in Vercel and call it done?

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix(auth): drop localhost fallback that broke production signup" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

The browser auth client was constructed with:

```ts
baseURL: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ?? "http://localhost:3000"
```

When deployed to Vercel without `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` set, the fallback meant every signup/sign-in POST went to `http://localhost:3000` from the visitor's browser — silently broken in production. This was reported on `agentcenter.vercel.app`.

Dropping `baseURL` entirely lets Better Auth's client fall back to the relative path `/api/auth`, which the browser resolves against `window.location.origin`. The client now works on any host without needing an env var.

Server-side auth (`lib/auth/index.ts`) and the share-URL composition in `extensions/[slug]/page.tsx` still use `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` — those are unaffected.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI: lint + typecheck + tests pass.
- [ ] Local: signup/sign-in still work at `http://localhost:3000` (relative URL resolves to the dev origin).
- [ ] Production: after merging + redeploying, signup works on `agentcenter.vercel.app` even with `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` unset (and continues to work if it is set, since dropping the option is host-agnostic).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

lastPrompt: find more chinese translation issues

content.text:
Found a handful of issues. Listing them so you can choose which to fix:

## Em-dash typography (definite)
Chinese style uses full-width `——` (joined, no surrounding spaces). The code uses ASCII `—` with spaces, which reads as Latin-style in Chinese text:

| Line | Current | Suggested |
|---|---|---|
| 55 | `请重试 —— 如果问题持续...` | `请重试——如果问题持续...` |
| 133 | `暂无评价 —— 成为第一个评价的人。` | `暂无评价——成为第一个评价的人。` |
| 405 | `已达上限 — 删除一个标签后可继续添加` | `已达上限——删除一个标签后可继续添加` |
| 416 | `请如实声明 — 用户安装前会看到这些权限。` | `请如实声明——用户安装前会看到这些权限。` |
| 429 | `等待管理员审核 — 通常 1 个工作日内完成` | `等待管理员审核——通常 1 个工作日内完成` |

## Wording (worth a look)

- **Line 132**: `"emptyReadme": "该扩展尚未发布 README 文档。"` — `发布` (publish) is awkward applied to README; `提供` ("provide") or `添加` reads better. (Same string at line 35.)
- **Line 416**: `"请如实声明"` — slightly stiff; `请如实勾选` reads more naturally for a permissions checkbox UI.

## Terminology consistency

- **Publisher**: filter UI uses `发布方` (`filters.publisher.label`, line 106), detail page uses `发布者` (`detail.publisher`, line 127). Both are valid Chinese but mean slightly different things — `发布者` is more common; `发布方` emphasizes "the party that publishes." Consider standardizing on one.

Want me to apply just the em-dash fixes, all of these, or pick specific ones?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

EOF
)"

content.text:
I'd go with **bundle** — it's already the codebase's canonical term (`extensionVersions.bundleFileId`, R2 "bundle storage" in CLAUDE.md, `publish.review.bundle: "Bundle/扩展包"`, `versionPendingUpload: "Needs bundle upload"`). Right now step 2 is the only place that drifts to "code/源代码", so renaming it actually fixes an existing inconsistency rather than introducing new jargon. Chinese has `扩展包` already established for the same concept.

Concrete proposed renames:

| Key | Current EN | New EN | Current ZH | New ZH |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sourceTitle` | Source | Bundle | 代码来源 | 扩展包 |
| `sourceDesc` | Bring your code into AgentCenter. | Bring your bundle into AgentCenter. | 把代码引入 AgentCenter。 | 把扩展包上传到 AgentCenter。 |
| `methodLabel` | How would you like to provide your code? | How would you like to provide your bundle? | 如何提供你的代码？ | 如何提供你的扩展包？ |

Tradeoff: "bundle" is mildly more technical than "code", but it's already the term users see two screens later (the bundle upload status, error messages, etc.), so consistency reduces cognitive load overall. Sound good?

lastPrompt: in the publish extension step 2, I think it is better to use package or bundle or others instead of code, what do you think? can you suggest the name?

502	export async function getDraft(extensionId: string): Promise<GetDraftResult> {
503	  const session = await auth.api.getSession({ headers: await headers() });
504	  if (!session) return { ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" };
505	
506	  const [row] = await db
507	    .select({
508	      extensionId: extensions.id,
509	      publisherUserId: extensions.publisherUserId,
510	      slug: extensions.slug,
511	      name: extensions.name,
512	      nameZh: extensions.nameZh,
513	      tagline: extensions.tagline,
514	      taglineZh: extensions.taglineZh,
515	      readmeMd: extensions.readmeMd,
516	      iconColor: extensions.iconColor,
517	      permissions: extensions.permissions,
518	      category: extensions.category,
519	      scope: extensions.scope,
520	      funcCat: extensions.funcCat,
521	      subCat: extensions.subCat,
522	      deptId: extensions.deptId,
523	      visibility: extensions.visibility,
524	      versionId: extensionVersions.id,
525	      version: extensionVersions.version,
526	      versionStatus: extensionVersions.status,
527	      bundleFileId: extensionVersions.bundleFileId,
528	      sourceMethod: extensionVersions.sourceMethod,
529	    })
530	    .from(extensions)
531	    .leftJoin(
532	      extensionVersions,
533	      eq(extensionVersions.extensionId, extensions.id),
534	    )
535	    .where(eq(extensions.id, extensionId))
536	    .orderBy(desc(extensionVersions.createdAt))
537	    .limit(1);
538	
539	  if (!row || !row.versionId || !row.version || !row.versionStatus) {
540	    return { ok: false, error: "not_found" };
541	  }
542	  if (row.publisherUserId !== session.user.id) {
543	    // Same shape as a missing row; see comment on `GetDraftResult`.
544	    return { ok: false, error: "not_found" };
545	  }
546	
547	  // Tag IDs in a separate, very small query — extensions cap tags at 8
548	  // per the form schema, so this is at most 8 rows.
549	  const tagRows = await db
550	    .select({ tagId: extensionTags.tagId })
551	    .from(extensionTags)
552	    .where(eq(extensionTags.extensionId, row.extensionId));
553	  const tagIds = tagRows.map((r) => r.tagId);
554	
555	  const iconColor = (row.iconColor ?? "indigo") as ManifestFormValues["iconColor"];
556	  const sourceMethod = (row.sourceMethod ?? "zip") as ManifestFormValues["sourceMethod"];
557	  const permissions = (row.permissions ?? {}) as ManifestFormValues["permissions"];
558	
559	  return {
560	    ok: true,
561	    draft: {
562	      extensionId: row.extensionId,
563	      versionId: row.versionId,
564	      slug: row.slug,
565	      version: row.version,
566	      name: row.name,
567	      category: row.category,
568	      scope: row.scope,
569	      visibility: row.visibility,
570	      versionStatus: row.versionStatus,
571	      bundleUploaded: row.bundleFileId !== null,
572	      formValues: {
573	        slug: row.slug,
574	        name: row.name,
575	        nameZh: row.nameZh ?? "",
576	        version: row.version,
577	        category: row.category as ManifestFormValues["category"],
578	        scope: row.scope as ManifestFormValues["scope"],
579	        deptId: row.deptId ?? "",
580	        tagIds,
581	        summary: row.tagline ?? "",

259	export async function getMyExtensions(userId: string) {
260	  // Pull the latest version per extension alongside the extension row so the
261	  // dashboard can show "where am I in the wizard" (e.g. needs upload vs.
262	  // submitted, awaiting scan) — visibility alone hides that info. Also
263	  // joins the bundle file via `bundleFileId` (logical reference, no FK)
264	  // so the dashboard can surface the scan rejection reason inline for
265	  // rejected versions without an extra round-trip.
266	  const rows = await db
267	    .select({
268	      id: extensions.id,
269	      slug: extensions.slug,
270	      name: extensions.name,
271	      category: extensions.category,
272	      visibility: extensions.visibility,
273	      createdAt: extensions.createdAt,
274	      latestVersionId: extensionVersions.id,
275	      latestVersion: extensionVersions.version,
276	      latestStatus: extensionVersions.status,
277	      latestBundleFileId: extensionVersions.bundleFileId,
278	      latestScanReport: files.scanReport,
279	    })
280	    .from(extensions)
281	    .leftJoin(
282	      extensionVersions,
283	      eq(extensionVersions.extensionId, extensions.id),
284	    )
285	    .leftJoin(files, eq(files.id, extensionVersions.bundleFileId))
286	    .where(eq(extensions.publisherUserId, userId))
287	    .orderBy(desc(extensions.createdAt), desc(extensionVersions.createdAt));
288	
289	  // Collapse to one row per extension (latest version first thanks to the
290	  // ORDER BY). If we someday support multiple in-flight versions per
291	  // extension, this becomes a `DISTINCT ON` query.
292	  const byExtension = new Map<
293	    string,
294	    (typeof rows)[number] & { latestScanReason: string | null }
295	  >();
296	  for (const row of rows) {
297	    if (!byExtension.has(row.id)) {
298	      byExtension.set(row.id, {
299	        ...row,
300	        latestScanReason: extractScanReason(row.latestScanReport),
301	      });
302	    }
303	  }
304	  return Array.from(byExtension.values());
305	}
306	
307	export type UpdateDraftResult =
308	  | { ok: true }

I can't reproduce by reading alone — the routing all checks out (`/publish/[id]/edit` exists, `getDraft` returns reasonable data, the wizard is a `"use client"` component that should hydrate fine). The "rendering" badge in the bottom-left is Next 16's segment-rendering indicator, so it appearing means the navigation *did* fire — something downstream is stalling or erroring.

To narrow it down, can you check your browser DevTools and your dev terminal:

1. **Browser console** — any red error rows after clicking a draft? (Hydration mismatch, unhandled rejection, etc.)
2. **Network tab** — what URL does the click request? Does the request finish (200 / 404 / pending forever)?
3. **Dev terminal** (where `next dev` is running) — any thrown stack traces around the time you click?

Paste anything that looks suspicious and I'll trace the cause. If nothing shows up, I'll spin up `next dev` here to repro.

content.text:
Looking at `top-bar.tsx:67-84`, the search bar has `max-w-[520px] flex-1` and sits right next to the logo, with a separate `flex-1` spacer at line 86 pushing the nav cluster to the right. On wide viewports that produces a layout like `[Menu Logo Search──────][gap-of-empty-space][Explore Publish Docs Θ 中 Avatar]` — the search visually clings to the left while the right side floats off alone.

I'd center it between the two clusters: replace the lone right-side spacer with a flex-1 wrapper around the search form using `justify-center`. The form keeps its 520px cap, but the empty space gets split evenly on both sides — the bar reads as a balanced three-section header (left logo cluster · centered search · right nav cluster) instead of left-heavy.

Want me to apply that?

content.text:
Honest take on this branch's changes:

- `i18n strings` (3 commits) — no tests needed; the suite doesn't assert on exact strings.
- `top-bar centering` — pure CSS, can't unit-test meaningfully.
- `trending category fix` — could add a `lib/search/query.test.ts` case for "no category → no category clause", but the existing tests already implicitly cover that by only adding a clause when the filter is set. Marginal value.

So no required tests for this branch. **However**, there's a real gap from PR #26 worth flagging: the creator/publisher filter additions shipped without tests — `buildExtensionWhere` doesn't have cases for `creator`/`publisher`, `filtersSchema` doesn't either, and `lib/db/queries/facets.ts` is uncovered. If we want to address it, it'd be a separate commit on this branch (or its own follow-up PR).

Three options:
1. Push this branch as-is and open the PR.
2. Add the creator/publisher filter test coverage on this branch first (one commit, ~30 min of test writing).
3. Push now, do the test coverage in a follow-up PR.

Which?

51:Server components render the static shell, fetch data via Drizzle, and stream HTML. Client components are reserved for interactive bits — filter chips, the install button, the upload wizard, the user menu — and are marked with `"use client"`. The pattern is "server frame, client islands."
53:The filter bar (`components/filters/filter-bar.tsx`) is a server component but composes client islands (`FilterChips`, `SortSelect`, `DeptPicker`, `TagDrawer`). Each island calls the typed `useFilters()` hook (`lib/hooks/use-filters.ts`), which exposes a parsed `filters` object and an `update(partial)` setter. Filter state lives in the URL — no global store, no context, and back/forward works. The hook delegates URL parsing/serializing to `parseFilters` / `serializeFilters` in `lib/validators/filters.ts` so the same conventions apply to both server-side reads (in RSCs) and client-side writes.
68:GET /en/extensions?category=skills&dept=eng.cloud&sort=stars
75:`buildExtensionWhere` / `buildExtensionOrder` in `lib/search/query.ts` compose Drizzle SQL clauses from filters; they're pure (no DB call), unit-tested, and shared with `/api/v1/extensions` so web and CLI see the same listing semantics.
145:The form is deliberately slimmer than the bundle TOML it eventually publishes. The wizard collects only what users edit interactively (name, slug, summary, type, scope, tags, dept, README, permissions, icon colour, source method); fields that the bundle's `manifest.toml` still requires (`description`, `funcCat`, `subCat`) get backfilled server-side. `extensions.funcCat`/`subCat` are nullable and `defaultClassification(category)` in `lib/actions/publish.ts` derives sensible defaults (`skills`/`plugins` → `tools/general`, `mcp` → `tools/integrations`, `slash` → `tools/commands`) on `createDraftExtension`. `updateDraftExtension` deliberately never writes those columns so admin curation isn't reverted on a draft re-save. New columns: `extensions.permissions` (jsonb of network/files/runtime/data toggles surfaced on the detail page) and `extension_versions.sourceMethod` / `sourceMeta` (text + jsonb — only `'zip'` is wired today; `'git'` and `'cli'` are reserved for future modes).

22:Web requests hit Vercel; the CLI hits the same Vercel deployment but talks only to the `/api/v1` surface. Bundles flow directly between the browser/CLI and R2 via signed URLs — Vercel never proxies binary content.
35:│   │   ├── page.tsx           # browse (filter bar + grid)
51:Server components render the static shell, fetch data via Drizzle, and stream HTML. Client components are reserved for interactive bits — filter chips, the install button, the upload wizard, the user menu — and are marked with `"use client"`. The pattern is "server frame, client islands."
53:The filter bar (`components/filters/filter-bar.tsx`) is a server component but composes client islands (`FilterChips`, `SortSelect`, `DeptPicker`, `TagDrawer`). Each island calls the typed `useFilters()` hook (`lib/hooks/use-filters.ts`), which exposes a parsed `filters` object and an `update(partial)` setter. Filter state lives in the URL — no global store, no context, and back/forward works. The hook delegates URL parsing/serializing to `parseFilters` / `serializeFilters` in `lib/validators/filters.ts` so the same conventions apply to both server-side reads (in RSCs) and client-side writes.
72:      └─ <FilterBar tags={tags} /> + <ExtGrid items={items} />
75:`buildExtensionWhere` / `buildExtensionOrder` in `lib/search/query.ts` compose Drizzle SQL clauses from filters; they're pure (no DB call), unit-tested, and shared with `/api/v1/extensions` so web and CLI see the same listing semantics.
105:The wizard is a 4-step rail layout (`<UploadWizard>` in `components/publish/wizard/`): **Basics → Source → Listing → Review**, with a sticky live-preview pane that mirrors the listing card and the derived `manifest.json`. Step 1 (Basics) creates the draft on advance; Steps 2-3 mutate it via `updateDraftExtension`; Step 4 submits.
112:   ├──── Source: upload bundle ─────│─── presigned PUT URL ───────►│             │
151:The dashboard at `/[locale]/publish` shows each draft's stage (`Needs bundle upload` / `Ready to submit` / `Awaiting scan` / etc., derived from `extensionVersions.status` + `bundleFileId` via `rowAction()` in `lib/publish/row-action.ts`). Rows whose latest version is `pending` are clickable and link into `/publish/[id]/edit`, which loads the draft via `getDraft()` and mounts the same `<UploadWizard>` in resume mode — Basics fields pre-filled, slug + version locked (because `extensionId` is set), and the initial step derived from whether a bundle is uploaded (no bundle → land on Source; bundle present → land on Review). Step transitions through Basics call `updateDraftExtension()` instead of `createDraftExtension()`; the action refuses (`version_not_editable`) once status leaves `pending`. Rejected rows aren't clickable but surface the scan reason inline (extracted from `files.scanReport` via `extractScanReason()` in `lib/publish/scan-report.ts`) so the dashboard isn't a dead end for failed scans.

content.content:
100	
101	The share URL on the hero is composed from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` rather than the request `Host` header, since spoofable forwarded headers would let an attacker control what gets copied to clipboard.
102	
103	### Publish
104	
105	The wizard is a 4-step rail layout (`<UploadWizard>` in `components/publish/wizard/`): **Basics → Source → Listing → Review**, with a sticky live-preview pane that mirrors the listing card and the derived `manifest.json`. Step 1 (Basics) creates the draft on advance; Steps 2-3 mutate it via `updateDraftExtension`; Step 4 submits.
106	
107	```text
108	Browser                          Vercel                          R2          Inngest
109	   │ Basics → Next                  │                              │             │
110	   ├──── createDraftExtension ────► server action  ──► extensions row (visibility=draft)
111	   │                                │                              │             │
112	   ├──── Source: upload bundle ─────│─── presigned PUT URL ───────►│             │
113	   │                                │                              │             │
114	   ├──── attachFile(versionId) ────► server action  ──► files row + extensionVersion
115	   │                                │                              │             │
116	   ├──── Listing/Review edits ─────► updateDraftExtension (sourceMethod, tags, README, …)
117	   │                                │                              │             │
118	   ├──── Review → Publish ─────────► submitForReview ──► sendEvent("extension/scan.requested")
119	   │                                │                              │             │
120	   │                                │                              │  scan-bundle:
121	   │                                │                              │   download from R2,
122	   │                                │                              │   sha-256 checksum,
123	   │                                │                              │   parse manifest.toml,
124	   │                                │                              │   validate schema,
125	   │                                │                              │   recordScanResult →
126	   │                                │                              │    scope=personal:
127	   │                                │                              │      version.status=ready
128	   │                                │                              │      + version.publishedAt=now
129	   │                                │                              │      + extensions.visibility=published
130	   │                                │                              │      + extensions.publishedAt=now
131	   │                                │                              │    scope=org|enterprise:
132	   │                                │                              │      version.status=ready
133	   │                                │                              │      (visibility stays draft —
134	   │                                │                              │       awaits admin publishVersion)
135	   │                                │                              │   sendEvent("extension/index.requested")
136	   │                                │                              │             │
137	   │                                │                              │  reindex-search:
138	   │                                │                              │   revalidateTag("extensions")
139	```
140	
141	The upload goes browser → R2 directly (presigned PUT) — Vercel only signs the URL. The Inngest webhook lives at `/api/inngest`. Job source: `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` and `lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts`.
142	
143	A note on `scan-bundle`: download + checksum + manifest parsing live in a single `step.run` rather than separate steps, because Inngest serializes step return values across boundaries and `Buffer` doesn't survive that round-trip.
144	
145	The form is deliberately slimmer than the bundle TOML it eventually publishes. The wizard collects only what users edit interactively (name, slug, summary, type, scope, tags, dept, README, permissions, icon colour, source method); fields that the bundle's `manifest.toml` still requires (`description`, `funcCat`, `subCat`) get backfilled server-side. `extensions.funcCat`/`subCat` are nullable and `defaultClassification(category)` in `lib/actions/publish.ts` derives sensible defaults (`skills`/`plugins` → `tools/general`, `mcp` → `tools/integrations`, `slash` → `tools/commands`) on `createDraftExtension`. `updateDraftExtension` deliberately never writes those columns so admin curation isn't reverted on a draft re-save. New columns: `extensions.permissions` (jsonb of network/files/runtime/data toggles surfaced on the detail page) and `extension_versions.sourceMethod` / `sourceMeta` (text + jsonb — only `'zip'` is wired today; `'git'` and `'cli'` are reserved for future modes).
146	
147	R2 bundle keys are `bundles/<slug>/<version>/bundle.zip` (`bundleKey()` in `lib/storage/r2.ts`). Two consequences worth knowing: (1) the wizard locks `slug` and `version` on the Basics step the moment a draft has been persisted server-side (i.e. once `extensionId` is set) — `updateDraftExtension` deliberately refuses to write either column, and an unlocked field would silently show edits the server will never persist or, worse, orphan the bundle at the old R2 key after upload; and (2) discarding a draft only deletes DB rows; the R2 object is left for bucket lifecycle to garbage-collect.
148	
149	### Resume, edit, and discard
150	
151	The dashboard at `/[locale]/publish` shows each draft's stage (`Needs bundle upload` / `Ready to submit` / `Awaiting scan` / etc., derived from `extensionVersions.status` + `bundleFileId` via `rowAction()` in `lib/publish/row-action.ts`). Rows whose latest version is `pending` are clickable and link into `/publish/[id]/edit`, which loads the draft via `getDraft()` and mounts the same `<UploadWizard>` in resume mode — Basics fields pre-filled, slug + version locked (because `extensionId` is set), and the initial step derived from whether a bundle is uploaded (no bundle → land on Source; bundle present → land on Review). Step transitions through Basics call `updateDraftExtension()` instead of `createDraftExtension()`; the action refuses (`version_not_editable`) once status leaves `pending`. Rejected rows aren't clickable but surface the scan reason inline (extracted from `files.scanReport` via `extractScanReason()` in `lib/publish/scan-report.ts`) so the dashboard isn't a dead end for failed scans.
152	
153	Each draft row also gets a `<DiscardButton>` that calls `discardDraft()` — owner-checked, draft-visibility-only, hard delete (FK cascades clean up versions/tags/files-row links). `getDraft` and `discardDraft` deliberately collapse non-owner into `not_found` so the public action contract can't be used to probe existence.
154

file.content:
The share URL on the hero is composed from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` rather than the request `Host` header, since spoofable forwarded headers would let an attacker control what gets copied to clipboard.

### Publish

The wizard is a 4-step rail layout (`<UploadWizard>` in `components/publish/wizard/`): **Basics → Source → Listing → Review**, with a sticky live-preview pane that mirrors the listing card and the derived `manifest.json`. Step 1 (Basics) creates the draft on advance; Steps 2-3 mutate it via `updateDraftExtension`; Step 4 submits.

```text
Browser                          Vercel                          R2          Inngest
   │ Basics → Next                  │                              │             │
   ├──── createDraftExtension ────► server action  ──► extensions row (visibility=draft)
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── Source: upload bundle ─────│─── presigned PUT URL ───────►│             │
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── attachFile(versionId) ────► server action  ──► files row + extensionVersion
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── Listing/Review edits ─────► updateDraftExtension (sourceMethod, tags, README, …)
   │                                │                              │             │
   ├──── Review → Publish ─────────► submitForReview ──► sendEvent("extension/scan.requested")
   │                                │                              │             │
   │                                │                              │  scan-bundle:
   │                                │                              │   download from R2,
   │                                │                              │   sha-256 checksum,
   │                                │                              │   parse manifest.toml,
   │                                │                              │   validate schema,
   │                                │                              │   recordScanResult →
   │                                │                              │    scope=personal:
   │                                │                              │      version.status=ready
   │                                │                              │      + version.publishedAt=now
   │                                │                              │      + extensions.visibility=published
   │                                │                              │      + extensions.publishedAt=now
   │                                │                              │    scope=org|enterprise:
   │                                │                              │      version.status=ready
   │                                │                              │      (visibility stays draft —
   │                                │                              │       awaits admin publishVersion)
   │                                │                              │   sendEvent("extension/index.requested")
   │                                │                              │             │
   │                                │                              │  reindex-search:
   │                                │                              │   revalidateTag("extensions")
```

The upload goes browser → R2 directly (presigned PUT) — Vercel only signs the URL. The Inngest webhook lives at `/api/inngest`. Job source: `lib/jobs/scan-bundle.ts` and `lib/jobs/reindex-search.ts`.

A note on `scan-bundle`: download + checksum + manifest parsing live in a single `step.run` rather than separate steps, because Inngest serializes step return values across boundaries and `Buffer` doesn't survive that round-trip.

The form is deliberately slimmer than the bundle TOML it eventually publishes. The wizard collects only what users edit interactively (name, slug, summary, type, scope, tags, dept, README, permissions, icon colour, source method); fields that the bundle's `manifest.toml` still requires (`description`, `funcCat`, `subCat`) get backfilled server-side. `extensions.funcCat`/`subCat` are nullable and `defaultClassification(category)` in `lib/actions/publish.ts` derives sensible defaults (`skills`/`plugins` → `tools/general`, `mcp` → `tools/integrations`, `slash` → `tools/commands`) on `createDraftExtension`. `updateDraftExtension` deliberately never writes those columns so admin curation isn't reverted on a draft re-save. New columns: `extensions.permissions` (jsonb of network/files/runtime/data toggles surfaced on the detail page) and `extension_versions.sourceMethod` / `sourceMeta` (text + jsonb — only `'zip'` is wired today; `'git'` and `'cli'` are reserved for future modes).

R2 bundle keys are `bundles/<slug>/<version>/bundle.zip` (`bundleKey()` in `lib/storage/r2.ts`). Two consequences worth knowing: (1) the wizard locks `slug` and `version` on the Basics step the moment a draft has been persisted server-side (i.e. once `extensionId` is set) — `updateDraftExtension` deliberately refuses to write either column, and an unlocked field would silently show edits the server will never persist or, worse, orphan the bundle at the old R2 key after upload; and (2) discarding a draft only deletes DB rows; the R2 object is left for bucket lifecycle to garbage-collect.

### Resume, edit, and discard

The dashboard at `/[locale]/publish` shows each draft's stage (`Needs bundle upload` / `Ready to submit` / `Awaiting scan` / etc., derived from `extensionVersions.status` + `bundleFileId` via `rowAction()` in `lib/publish/row-action.ts`). Rows whose latest version is `pending` are clickable and link into `/publish/[id]/edit`, which loads the draft via `getDraft()` and mounts the same `<UploadWizard>` in resume mode — Basics fields pre-filled, slug + version locked (because `extensionId` is set), and the initial step derived from whether a bundle is uploaded (no bundle → land on Source; bundle present → land on Review). Step transitions through Basics call `updateDraftExtension()` instead of `createDraftExtension()`; the action refuses (`version_not_editable`) once status leaves `pending`. Rejected rows aren't clickable but surface the scan reason inline (extracted from `files.scanReport` via `extractScanReason()` in `lib/publish/scan-report.ts`) so the dashboard isn't a dead end for failed scans.

Each draft row also gets a `<DiscardButton>` that calls `discardDraft()` — owner-checked, draft-visibility-only, hard delete (FK cascades clean up versions/tags/files-row links). `getDraft` and `discardDraft` deliberately collapse non-owner into `not_found` so the public action contract can't be used to probe existence.

Each island calls the typed `useFilters()` hook (`lib/hooks/use-filters.ts`), which exposes a parsed `filters` object and an `update(partial)` setter. Filter state lives in the URL — no global store, no context, and back/forward works. gh pr create --title "Copy polish, top-bar centering, trending fix, filter test coverage" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Grab-bag of small fixes I picked up while testing the merged PR #26 work.

- **`fix(home)`** — the home "Trending" row was hardcoded to `category: "skills"` even though the heading was generic. MCP/slash/plugins never showed up on the home page. Drop the category filter and rename the heading to plain "Trending" / "热门".
- **`fix(top-bar)`** — the search input clung to the left next to the logo with a wide empty gap before the right nav cluster. Wrap the form in `flex flex-1 justify-center` so the empty space splits evenly; the search keeps its 520px cap and now reads as the centered third of a balanced 3-column header.
- **`chore(i18n)` × 2** — Chinese polish (`斜线命令` typo → `斜杠命令`, `发布方/发布者` → `发布商`, em-dashes converted to CJK-style `——`, README empty-state wording, permissions hint), plus renaming publish step 2 from `Source / 代码来源` to `Bundle / 扩展包` (already the codebase's term for the artifact — `bundleFileId`, R2 "bundle storage", etc.). Step 2 was the only place still calling it "code".
- **`test(filters)`** — backfills test coverage for the creator/publisher filter additions from PR #26, which shipped without tests. +7 tests on `filtersSchema`, the parse/serialize round-trip, and `buildExtensionWhere` (292 → 299).
- **`docs(architecture)`** — refreshes two stale references: the filter-bar island list (now includes `PublisherPicker` + `CreatorPicker`) and the wizard step name (`Basics → Bundle → Listing → Review`).

## Test plan

- [ ] CI: lint + typecheck + tests pass.
- [ ] Browse `/` — Trending grid shows extensions across all four categories, heading reads "Trending" / "热门".
- [ ] Top bar on a wide viewport — search input visually centered between logo cluster and right nav.
- [ ] Switch to `/zh` and skim through the publish wizard, error states, and the README empty state — em-dashes look right (no leading spaces), `发布商` reads consistently in filter UI and detail page, step 2 title reads `扩展包`.
- [ ] Open `/extensions` — Creator and Publisher pickers still work.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

I will publish a daily report about AgentCenter itself. It should contain a complete feature table including main features, sub-features, and other improvements, along with a daily briefing. The daily briefing can begin today. what do you think?

content.text:
Two open questions before I'd recommend a shape:

1. **Where does it live?** Two reasonable options: (a) markdown files in `docs/` (`docs/features.md` + `docs/daily-log.md` — committed, AI-navigable, easy diffing), or (b) actual pages in the app (`/changelog` route, public-facing). I'd start with (a) — zero infrastructure, lets you iterate on what to track before committing to a UI; promote to (b) later if you want it public.
2. **Two files or one?** I'd split: a feature inventory you *overwrite* as the source-of-truth ("what works today, organized by area") and a daily briefing you *append* to ("what changed on YYYY-MM-DD"). Single file conflates state with log and gets unwieldy fast.

Today's first briefing has good material to seed it — three PRs landed (#26 home/filters, #27 auth fix, #28 copy/bundle/trending). Want me to scaffold both files with that structure and today's entry?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: for the two questions, what is your opinion?

content.text:
1. **`docs/` markdown** — zero infra, already where the project's other docs live (architecture.md, plan.md), evolves alongside code through normal PRs. Promoting to an in-app `/changelog` page later is cheap if you want it public; getting the content right first is what matters.
2. **Two files**. The feature inventory and the daily log have different lifecycles — one is "current state" (overwritten/edited) and the other is "history" (append-only). Mixing them means you rewrite the wrong thing half the time and the file grows unfocused.

Want me to go scaffold `docs/features.md` and `docs/daily-log.md` with today's first entry?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: ok, and I want it to be bi-lingual, English and Chinese version. how about that?

content.text:
Mirror the project's existing pattern of parallel files (`messages/en.json` + `messages/zh.json`): `docs/features.md` + `docs/features.zh.md`, same for daily-log. Reads cleanly in either language, matches GitHub convention (`README.zh.md`), and avoids the side-by-side mode where each file is half-readable in either language.

Tradeoff: you'll be writing every entry twice. If that's the deal-breaker we can do single-file with English first, Chinese second per section — but I'd recommend pushing through the duplication for the cleaner result. Want me to scaffold all four files now?

content.text:
Works — and probably the right call given you'll be updating these regularly. Two files, each bilingual:

- **`docs/features.md`** — feature inventory as a table with bilingual columns: `| Area | Feature (EN) | 功能 | Status | Notes |`. Tables stay compact even with two languages.
- **`docs/daily-log.md`** — appended per day. Each date entry has an EN section then a 中文 section underneath, separated by a `---`. Skimmable in either language without forcing the other to mid-page.

Want me to scaffold both with today's first entry (PRs #26, #27, #28)?
